SCCCI EVENTS USABILITY TEST
PARTICIPANT PACK · PLEASE READ BEFORE YOUR SESSION

Welcome to your
usability session.

Thank you for helping us test CERTAIN, an event management system for SCCCI events. Inside this pack: the two roles you will step into, and the six scenarios you will act out. Read time is about 8 minutes. This pack does not include prototype access — you will only receive it when your interview starts, so your first reactions stay genuine. Remember: we are testing the system, never you. There are no wrong answers, and you may stop at any time.
SESSION LENGTH ≈ 50 MIN · 6 SCENARIOS × 5 MIN EACH SCREEN + AUDIO + FACE (WEBCAM) RECORDED WITH YOUR PDPA CONSENT
BEFORE WE START · WHAT IS CERTAIN?

The system you'll be trying out.

CERTAIN is an event management system being designed for SCCCI (Singapore Chinese Chamber of Commerce & Industry) events — large business conferences with hundreds of delegates arriving at once. Instead of queueing at a registration desk with a printed confirmation, delegates check in with facial recognition and get instant answers from an AI chatbot, while event staff run gates, headcounts and emergencies from a command centre.
THE PROBLEM IT TRIES TO SOLVE
At past events, delegates faced long check-in queues, staff couldn't verify identities quickly, and questions like "where is my seminar room?" piled up at a single help desk. CERTAIN aims to make arrival hands-free and instant — but only if real people find it easy to use. That's where you come in.
WHAT YOU'LL SEE ON THE DAY
Delegate app — face check-in, event schedule, AI chatbot, biometrics settings
Staff command centre — scanner setup, live headcounts, identity checks, AI protocol assistant
It is a clickable prototype, not a finished product — some buttons are simulated. If something feels broken or confusing, that is exactly what we want to hear.
NO PRIOR KNOWLEDGE NEEDED · WE ARE TESTING THE DESIGN, NOT YOU · THERE ARE NO WRONG ANSWERS
HOW YOUR SESSION WORKS

Two roles, one system.

Every participant tries all six scenarios — first three as a delegate, then three as event staff. This keeps the test fair and unskewed: everyone sees both sides of CERTAIN. Before each scenario, the moderator reads you a short story that sets the scene — you don't need to remember anything from this pack.
ROLE 1 · THE DELEGATE SCENARIOS D1–D3
A business guest attending an SCCCI conference
You've registered for a large business event. On your phone you'll use the CERTAIN delegate app to: check in with your face instead of queueing at a desk, find your way to the right seminar room, ask the AI chatbot questions, and manage your facial-recognition privacy settings.
FACE CHECK-IN · SCHEDULE · CHATBOT · BIOMETRICS
ROLE 2 · EVENT STAFF SCENARIOS S1–S3
The team running the event behind the scenes
Now you're on the other side of the door, using the CERTAIN staff command centre: set up the face scanners before doors open, watch live headcounts, verify a delegate's identity when something doesn't match, and consult the AI protocol assistant during an emergency.
SCANNER SETUP · HEADCOUNT · IDENTITY · AI PROTOCOL
NO EXPERIENCE IN EITHER ROLE IS NEEDED — THE SCENARIOS GIVE YOU ALL THE CONTEXT ON THE DAY
SCENARIO D1

Walk-up face check-in

DELEGATE PORTAL FACE ID ⏱ 5 MIN
👤
SCENARIO CONTEXT
You have just arrived at the SCCCI event venue with hand luggage and a coat draped over your arm. A queue of delegates is forming at the entrance and staff are directing everyone to the CERTAIN kiosks. Your conference badge is buried in your bag. You open the CERTAIN app on your phone and are ready to check in for the Opening Ceremony.
🎯
USER GOAL
Check in for the Opening Ceremony using only your face — no badge, no QR code, no digging through your bag. You want the confirmation screen in under 60 seconds so you can join the queue moving into the hall.
📋
TASK INSTRUCTION
  • Open CERTAIN and navigate to the check-in section from the homepage
  • Select the Opening Ceremony event to check in for
  • Choose Facial Recognition as your check-in method
  • Follow the on-screen steps to complete the face scan (in this prototype, the “Simulate Face Recognition” button stands in for the physical kiosk scanner)
  • Confirm that you have been successfully checked in and identify your next step
SCENARIO D2

AI chatbot · self-serve & escalate

DELEGATE PORTAL AI CHATBOT ⏱ 5 MIN
👤
SCENARIO CONTEXT
You have settled into the venue but have two urgent questions: whether today's lunch is halal, and what time your shuttle departs tonight. Staff nearby are occupied managing the crowd. You decide to use the CERTAIN AI Chatbot to get answers without queuing for a human. During the conversation, you realise one of your older chat threads was started by mistake and you want to delete it from your history.
🎯
USER GOAL
Get answers to your questions without asking a staff member — and when the chatbot can't fully resolve a specific dietary issue, escalate it to a real human using the Submit Ticket flow.
📋
TASK INSTRUCTION
  • Open the AI Chatbot from the CERTAIN home screen
  • Ask the chatbot about halal food options at today's lunch
  • Ask a follow-up question about your shuttle departure time
  • Attempt to delete the mistaken chat from your Chat History
  • For an issue the chatbot cannot resolve, find the Submit Ticket option and send it
  • After submitting, say aloud how confident you are that it reached a human staff member
SCENARIO D3

Update biometrics & switch event

DELEGATE PORTAL BIOMETRICS · MAP ⏱ 5 MIN
👤
SCENARIO CONTEXT
You recently changed your glasses and are worried the CERTAIN facial scanner won't recognise you correctly at tomorrow's VIP Gala Dinner. You want to update your face data proactively before the event. After updating, you also need to find the scanner location for the Gala — which is a different event to the Opening Ceremony you already checked in for.
🎯
USER GOAL
Update your face biometrics with confidence — understanding the difference between updating and deleting your data — then switch to the VIP Gala event and find the correct scanner location on the venue map.
📋
TASK INSTRUCTION
  • Navigate to your profile and find the Facial Biometrics settings
  • Understand the difference between Update and Unregister before acting
  • Update your biometric data (the re-scan is simulated in this prototype)
  • Read and understand the on-device privacy notice
  • Switch your active event from Opening Ceremony to the VIP Gala Dinner
  • Locate the Gala scanner on the map and confirm the route
SCENARIO S1

Set up face-scanner session

STAFF PORTAL SCANNER SETUP ⏱ 5 MIN
👤
SCENARIO CONTEXT
It is 45 minutes before the Opening Ceremony. You are a junior coordinator responsible for activating the facial recognition scanner at Gate A. The senior coordinator has sent you to the CERTAIN staff portal to assign the scanner session to the Opening Ceremony and enable Auto-scan before the first delegate arrives. You have used the portal twice before but have never set up a scanner session solo.
🎯
USER GOAL
Assign the correct scanner session to the Opening Ceremony event and confirm that Auto-scan is correctly enabled — all before the first delegate queue forms at Gate A in 45 minutes.
📋
TASK INSTRUCTION
  • Open the CERTAIN Staff Portal and navigate to Scanner Setup
  • Select the Opening Ceremony from the event list
  • Review the session duration and Auto-scan settings before assigning
  • Enable Auto-scan for the session
  • Assign the scanner session to the event and confirm it is active
  • Verify the session status shows the correct event and gate
SCENARIO S2

Identity verification & missing VIP override

STAFF PORTAL HEADCOUNT · FACE ⏱ 5 MIN
👤
SCENARIO CONTEXT
Peak arrival is underway. A delegate approaches the scanner — the system returns a 99.4% face-match confidence score, but the Identification Log below shows a “Not Registered” entry for this event. Two minutes later, your radio crackles: the bus departs in 2 minutes but the headcount shows fewer boarded than expected, and a VIP delegate is unaccounted for. You must act fast — without stalling the entire queue or delaying the coach.
🎯
USER GOAL
Verify delegates accurately against the full identity record (not just the confidence score), then under real time pressure, isolate the missing VIP from the Live Headcount and override their attendance deliberately.
📋
TASK INSTRUCTION
  • Review the face-match result — check both the confidence score and the Identification Log entry
  • Confirm or deny access for the current delegate based on the full record
  • Navigate to Live Headcount and filter by Missing and VIP
  • Identify the missing VIP from the filtered list
  • Use the Override function to manually mark them as present
  • Verify the delegate now shows as accounted in the headcount
SCENARIO S3

AI protocol query & VIP incident

STAFF PORTAL AI PROTOCOL ⏱ 5 MIN
👤
SCENARIO CONTEXT
The VIP shuttle bus has broken down 2 km from the venue with 12 delegates and a foreign dignitary on board. Your senior coordinator is in a closed meeting and you must act immediately. You open the CERTAIN AI Protocol Assistant to retrieve the official Missing Delegate / VIP Incident protocol. The AI returns a step-by-step guidance response — but you notice a small footer text below it. You must decide: act on the AI guidance immediately, or verify it first?
🎯
USER GOAL
Retrieve the correct VIP incident protocol from the AI Protocol Assistant, critically evaluate how much to trust the AI's guidance, escalate to a supervisor via the system, and activate the appropriate emergency transport protocol — all within the time window before the dignitary's schedule is disrupted.
📋
TASK INSTRUCTION
  • Open the AI Protocol Assistant from the Staff Portal
  • Query it for the VIP Bus Breakdown / Incident Protocol
  • Read the AI's response — including any disclaimers or footer notes
  • Decide whether to act on the AI guidance immediately or verify it first
  • Use CERTAIN to escalate the incident to a supervisor
  • Identify and initiate the emergency transport protocol for the stranded delegates

Your feedback will help identify usability issues and improve the overall experience. You are not being tested — the system is.

1.
Simple, Clear Language
★

2.
No Right or Wrong Answers
★
This is not a test of your abilities. We are evaluating the system, not you. Please interact as naturally as possible.
3.
Testing Procedures & Recording
★
The session may be observed and screen + audio + your face (webcam) recorded for educational purposes. All information stays strictly confidential.
4.
Encourage Honest Feedback
★
Please share your thoughts openly. Think aloud while completing tasks and explain any confusion or difficulty encountered.
5.
Questions & Concerns
★
You may stop the session at any time. If you have any questions or concerns, please inform the moderator immediately.
PDPA DATA & PRIVACY NOTICE
In compliance with Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA), please note:
• Screen, audio & face (webcam) recordings are collected for educational analysis only • No biometric data is processed by the prototype — face-scan screens are a Figma simulation • All recordings are deleted after analysis and never shared externally • You may withdraw at any time without penalty or explanation Questions? Before you arrive.

1 · Read the two roles. You’ll experience CERTAIN as a delegate first, then as event staff — the "Two roles, one system" page explains both. No acting skill needed: just be yourself.
2 · Skim all six scenarios. D1–D3 as a delegate, S1–S3 as staff — everyone does all six. Just the story; you do not need to memorise any steps.
3 · Think aloud on the day. Say what you see, what you expect, and what confuses you — your running commentary is the most valuable data we collect.
4 · After your session — the SUS questionnaire. Right after your last scenario, you’ll fill in a short 10-question System Usability Scale form (~2 minutes) at forms.gle/g4ymURsZpF4JvWtG6 — answer with your first instinct; there are no right or wrong answers.
5 · Sign the Participant Consent Sheet.

Please confirm you have read this entire document and are comfortable being part of this research. We will then ask you to sign the consent sheet on arrival.

We record your screen, audio and face (webcam) — recordings are used only for analysis within the project team and will not be shared externally.

⚠ You may withdraw at any time without penalty or consequence.
QUESTIONS? CONTACT THE MODERATOR · RIHAN IQBAL · NANYANG POLYTECHNIC · IT2114
